Motion And Notice Of Hearingplaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendant Tony Fu'S Motion For Order Declaring William Mora A Vexatious Litigant, Request For Prefiling Order, And To Furnish Security (Motions)
Off Calendar Jul-08-2022 Continued To Jul-13-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 303
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF WILLIAM MORA's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendant Tony Fu'S Motion For Order Declaring William Mora A Vexatious Litigant, Request For Prefiling Order, And To Furnish Security.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The matter is transferred to be heard in department 303 before Judge Bolanos on July 13, 2022 at 9:30 a.m.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-17-2022 Continued To Aug-17-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Tony Fu Motion For Order Declaring William Mora A Vexatious Litigant, Request For Prefiling Order, And Request For Order Of Dismissal Or To Furnish Security (Motions) For The Purposes Of Tentative Ruling Only. (Part 2 Of 2 Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar May-27-2022 Off Calendar

Motion For Order Declaring William Mora A Vexatious Litigant, Request For Prefiling Order, And Request For Order Of Dismissal Or To Furnish Security (Motions)
Off Calendar May-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TONY FU's Motion For Order Declaring William Mora A Vexatious Litigant, Request For Prefiling Order, And Request For Order Of Dismissal Or To Furnish Security.

Defendant Fu's motion to declare plaintiff William Mora a vexatious litigant is granted.

CCP 391(b)(1) provides a vexatious litigant is one who "[i]n the immediately preceding seven-year period has commenced, prosecuted, or maintained in propria persona at least five litigations other than in a small claims court that have been (i) finally determined adversely to the person or (ii) unjustifiably permitted to remain pending at least two years without have been brought to trial or hearing." "The term 'litigation' is broadly defined in section 391, subdivision (a) as meaning 'any civil action or proceeding, commenced, maintained or pending in any state or federal court'" and includes appeals. (Fink v. Shemtov (2010) 180 Cal.App.4th 1160, 1170.) A dismissal on the merits is not necessary. (See Tokerud v. Capitalbank Sacramento (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 775, 779 [vexatious litigant statute applies to voluntary dismissals].)

In this case, defendant cites five such litigations within the past seven years. Those cases are:

1. William Mora v. Bryant Fu, cross-action of Bryant Fu v. Mei Ling Fang, et al., San Francisco Superior Court, case no. CGC-18-572347, judgment entered against Mora on May 4, 2021. (Statement of Decision at Fu Decl., Exhibit E)

2. William Mora v. Bryant Fu, San Francisco Superior Court, case no. CGC-20-
583002, order of involuntary dismissal on April 7, 2021. (Fu Decl., Exhibit F)

3. William Mora v. Bryant Fu, San Francisco Superior Court, case no. CGC-18-
568890, complaint stricken and ordered involuntarily dismissed on September 20,
2018. (Fu Decl., Exhibit G)

4. City of Las Vegas v. William Mora, New Mexico District Court, Las Vegas District, appeal from court case no. D-412-LR-201500002, order of dismissal filed on December 1, 2016, order denying petition filed May 2, 2017. (Fu Decl., Exhibit H)

5. City of Las Vegas v. William Mora, New Mexico District Court, Las Vegas District, appeal, case no. D-412-CV-201300060, ordered dismissed on May 5, 2015. (Fu Decl., Exhibit I).

Moreover, defendant's evidence-including the photographs showing plaintiff following defendant-demonstrate that plaintiff has no reasonable probability of prevailing in this litigation.

Pursuant to CCP 391.3(a), plaintiff shall furnish a security for $20,000 to continue to maintain this action. The security shall be posted by June 30, 2022. Plaintiff's failure to provide the security in a timely fashion will result in a dismissal of this action. (See CCP 391.4.) Plaintiff is barred from commencing new litigation in the courts of this state in propria persona unless the Presiding Judge of the court where the litigation is to be filed provides leave to do so. (See CCP 391.7.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)
